message:
test: pass explicit cx to server startup / allow mut harness in mcp conformance

Two integration tests had to keep up with the cx-plumbing refactor of
the MCP server harness. No production code is modified.

- tests/robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs — the two test bodies
  `robot_sdk_transport_integration_get_text_roundtrip` and
  `robot_sdk_transport_integration_preserves_robot_error_semantics`
  spawn a server via the RPC entrypoint. Switches the spawn from the
  old `server.run_with_registry_auth_and_rpc(...)` to the new
  `server.run_with_registry_auth_and_rpc_with_cx(&server_cx, ...)`
  form, threading `server_cx =
  frankenterm_core::cx::for_testing()` into the spawned task. The
  non-cx variant is being removed in favor of always carrying a
  cancellation / observability cx through server lifecycles.

- tests/wa_event_mutations_mcp_conformance.rs — `capture_tool_contract`
  builds a `boundary_harness` and then calls
  `boundary_harness.client.<tool>(...)`, which the harness now
  exposes through a method that requires a `&mut self`. Changes the
  local binding to `let mut boundary_harness = new_harness();` so the
  mutable call compiles. Also adds `CompatRuntime` to the
  `runtime_compat::{...}` import list because a spawn call further
  down in the file now uses `CompatRuntime::spawn_blocking` rather
  than the top-level `runtime_compat::task::spawn_blocking` helper.
